sprint: Creator Signature Tap Board — Legible, Kid-Friendly Rework
task: A real user-supplied screenshot showed the "Lumo, the Gatekeeper" constellation tap challenge with almost every one of its 100 grid cells fully invisible -- "fix this screen also. very difficult to show the stars," followed by a direct product correction mid-fix: "think kids. its not easy for kids. they need fun not a test." Root cause: only a sparse ~22% subset of cells (js/magicCardUI.js's _gateBuildGrid, the --decoy class) ever showed any glow at all -- the remaining ~78% were fully color:transparent, so on a touch device (no :hover at all before the tap itself) a kid had no visual cue where most of the 100 tappable positions even were. Fixed by making every cell visible, uniformly, at rest -- the sparse decoy/invisible split is removed entirely, and css/style.css's base cell rule changed from color:transparent/font-size:15px to a real, legible color:rgba(255,255,255,.34) with a soft text-shadow glow and font-size:20px -- a genuine improvement on the original "camouflage" idea, not a trade-off against it: with every cell now identical at rest, there's no visible subset left to distinguish from "real" positions, so a shoulder-surfer watching a real tap sequence still can't tell a genuine tap from the rest of the now-uniformly-dim starfield around it. Added a real :active state alongside the existing :hover, since touch devices often skip :hover entirely. The board's own width cap grew from 460px to 520px for bigger, friendlier touch targets. Each cell also gained a gentle, randomized twinkle (opacity keyframe, independent of the color alpha channel, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion). The "fun not a test" correction went beyond legibility into the interaction and copy itself: every subtitle on this screen dropped "in order" -- a real, previously-unnoticed correctness gap, not just a tone fix, since both the local pattern check and the real recall_magic_card() RPC have always compared taps as an unordered SET, never a sequence; and the button/status copy was softened from clinical pass/fail wording to this screen's own established "sky" language -- "Confirm" became "That's My Sky!", "Checking..." became "Looking for your sky...", and the harsh X mark on a wrong guess was removed entirely. Verified via a new 16-assertion Playwright suite confirming all 100 cells render with real, identical, non-zero rest opacity, the real tap-select/connecting-line/counter mechanics still work end to end, the softened copy is present with no X mark, and the twinkle animation carries genuinely randomized per-cell timing. Full regression across the entire existing Gateway/Magic-Card Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected) pass unchanged, zero console errors; one pre-existing test's own text-based button selector needed updating to the stable CSS class -- the intended, disclosed consequence of the copy rename, not a regression. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/magicCardUI.js and css/style.css.
